beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Full energy start to the week; quick 30-min morning routine before heading out on this sunny day.

beats.activity: Drive to Kirkland Public Library branch.
beats.reasoning: Commute from Sand Point to Kirkland across Lake Washington; 40 min accounts for bridge traffic on a Monday morning.

beats.activity: Work shift: shelving, reference desk, patron assistance.
beats.reasoning: Standard work morning; Layla is not interested in the 11am USA–Wales match, so she stays focused on her shift despite bars opening early downtown.

beats.activity: Lunch break: quick bite at a local cafe near the library.
beats.reasoning: Takes a proper lunch break rather than watching the match; prefers quiet, focused time to big social crowds.

beats.activity: Afternoon work shift: cataloging, programming prep, and community engagement.
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon shift; finishing the workday before heading home in good weather.
Return commute across Lake Washington; 50 min to account for early-evening traffic on a sunny Monday.

beats.activity: Quick errand: pick up groceries and supplies for evening pottery project.
beats.reasoning: Stops near home in Ravenna to grab supplies; takes advantage of the sunny evening before settling in for a creative evening.

beats.activity: Dinner: preparing a simple meal at home.
beats.reasoning: Cooking at home to fuel the evening; introverted preference for quiet meal after a full workday.

beats.activity: Pottery work and tending to houseplants in the back garden.; Wind down: reading and light tidying at home.
beats.reasoning: Evening creative time in good weather; checks in on named plants and works on pottery—two core passions that align with her mood and the sunny day.
7.5-hour sleep overnight; full recovery after a productive, sunny Monday of work and creative evening.

diary: Woke to that perfect early-June light streaming through the bedroom—the kind that makes you remember why people move to Seattle. Coffee tasted better than usual, probably because I knew the weather would hold. The drive to Kirkland was smooth, which felt like a small victory given the Light Rail mess downtown. Work was steady: the usual shuffle of shelving and reference questions, though I caught myself distracted during the morning shift, thinking about the World Cup match everyone was buzzing about at Café Veloce. Didn't watch it—never been my thing—but the energy was hard to miss. Afternoon flew by with cataloging and prepping for next month's community programming. By the time I got home and hit Safeway, my shoulders were tight. But then I got my hands in the clay for a couple hours, shaped something without overthinking it, watered the ferns. There's something about pottery that untangles the day. Finished with a book and some quiet. Felt good.
thought_process: I decided to lean into routine today rather than fight it—the Light Rail closure meant I stuck with driving, which actually gave me more control over my morning. The World Cup buzz downtown was infectious even though I'm not invested; I think that's why I stayed focused on work instead of trying to catch the match somewhere. Around mid-afternoon, I felt the weight of being in systems all day (cataloging, patron requests, schedules), so I made sure to carve out real time with the pottery and plants when I got home. The weather helped my mood—that sunshine felt like permission to slow down. Small choices, but they added up to feeling grounded by evening.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
